lessons.text: Story sent back ('Tonight in Saline Twp: Data Center Update on agenda'): we need back story on this. what does the agenda say? what's inside? we need details with a headline that will make me care even 30 minutes away. thanks

Story sent back ('Democratic Senate candidates clash over AI data center regulation in d'): too many social posts.. we only need a few a day.. also i'm looking for social posts from political leaders with comments.. and a mix of democrat and republican

Story killed ('Haley Stevens non-answer on data centers prompts essay reference'): too many social posts.. we only need a few a day.. also i'm looking for social posts from political leaders with comments.. and a mix of democrat and republican
Story sent back ('John James criticized for data center builder donations amid attacks'): the person with the x post is not a big name.. just a random rant it seems so we will not be using it.

Story sent back ('Saline Twp Board Agenda Includes Data Center Update'): what is the back story? what happened at the last meeting? research other media reports. write this is a little news story. the word update should never be in the headline. find me what the story is actually about and incorporate the stakes and impac

Story killed ('Dowagiac Residents Report Persistent Noise From Nearby Data Center Dis'): we cannot link to this story because they are linking to an mlive report. this is not vice's original reporting.
Story killed ('U.S. Data Center Power Demand Surges as Tech Giants Accelerate AI Infr'): this is a fake news story. the link to the source does not work. double check.
Story killed ('Greenwood County Holds Public Hearing on Data Center Regulations'): this is hyperlocal national news.. does not impact michigan

Story sent back ('Delta County Planning Commission Backs 12-Month Data Center Moratorium'): this headline is too similar to the story. we always need context. why would be 60 miles away care? the headlines must be rewritten but be journalistically correct.
Story killed ('Lawmakers in 20+ States Propose Rules on Data Centers Amid Local Pushb'): this story is from yesterday. i am only looking for stories from the past 8-12 hours. remember that.

Story sent back ('Saline Board to Review IFEC Application on Data Center Project'): That headline is very boring and is a bunch of meeting jargon.. what does this mean for people in the area.. what's on the line? we shouldn't not speak in meeting-speak. we need actual news headlines that are attention grabbing

Story sent back ('Saline Twp Board Weighs IFEC Application for Data Center Project'): That headline is very boring and is a bunch of meeting jargon.. what does this mean for people in the area.. what's on the line? we shouldn't not speak in meeting-speak. we need actual news headlines that are attention grabbing

rules.rule: Only select Michigan-focused stories with clear local impact; ignore hyperlocal national news.
examples: Greenwood County Holds Public Hearing on Data Center Regulations

rules.rule: Only use stories from the past 8-12 hours; discard older items.
examples: Lawmakers in 20+ States Propose Rules on Data Centers

rules.rule: Require original reporting or direct sourcing; reject links to other outlets without added value.
examples: Dowagiac Residents Report Persistent Noise From Nearby Data Center

rules.rule: Headlines must include context, stakes, and impact; never use the word 'update'.
examples: Saline Twp Board Agenda Includes Data Center Update

rules.rule: Limit to a few posts per day; source only from notable political leaders with balanced D/R mix.
examples: Democratic Senate candidates clash over AI data center regulation

rules.rule: Verify all links and sources before use; reject non-functional or fabricated references.
examples: U.S. Data Center Power Demand Surges as Tech Giants Accelerate AI Infr

rules.rule: Ignore random or non-notable social media rants; only quote established voices.
examples: John James criticized for data center builder donations amid attacks
